description: Main economic definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: economics
words.description: social science that studies the production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services

words.name: social science
words.description: academic disciplines concerned with society and the relationships between individuals in society

words.name: microeconomics
words.description: branch of economics that studies the behavior of individual households and firms in making decisions on the allocation of limited resources

words.name: macroeconomics
words.description: branch of economics that studies aggregated indicators

words.name: econometrics
words.description: academic discipline

words.name: behavioral economics
words.description: discipline of economy studying the effects of psychological, cognitive, emotional, cultural and social factors on decisions

words.name: international economics
words.description: academic discipline

words.name: public economics
words.description: study of government economic and fiscal policy

words.name: labour economics
words.description: functioning and dynamics of the markets for labour

words.name: financial economics
words.description: branch of economics concerned with financial or monetary transactions

words.name: monetary economics
words.description: branch of economics that studies different theories of money

words.name: development economics
words.description: economics of developing economies

words.name: supply
words.description: in economics, the amount of a good that sellers are willing to provide in the market

words.name: demand
words.description: in economics, the market force that causes buyers to be both willing and able to purchase a good or service, measured by the amount of that good or service that is currently salable at any given price point

words.name: price
words.description: quantity of payment or compensation given by one party to another in return for goods or services; the proposed value for such a quantity as proposed by the seller

words.name: market
words.description: mechanisms whereby supply and demand confront each other and deals are made, involving places, processes and institutions in which exchanges occur (for physical venues, use Q132510 or Q330284)

words.name: economic equilibrium
words.description: state where economic forces such as supply and demand are balanced and the values of economic variables will not change

words.name: elasticity
words.description: responsiveness of one economic variable to a change in another (e.g. supply or demand as a function of changed price, cross-price, income, wealth, advertising, etc.), ranging from inelastic to elastic

words.name: price elasticity of demand
words.description: responsiveness of the quantity demanded of a good or service to a change in its price

words.name: price elasticity of supply
words.description: responsiveness of the quantity supplied of a good or service to a change in its price

words.name: income elasticity of demand
words.description: responsiveness of demand for goods with respect to income change

words.name: cross elasticity of demand
words.description: responsiveness in the quantity demand of one good when a change in price takes place in another good

words.name: utility
words.description: concept in economics & game theory: a relative measure of happiness or satisfaction

words.name: marginal utility
words.description: change in satisfaction resulting from an increase or decrease in consumption of one unit of a good or service

words.name: economic surplus
words.description: monetary benefit that accrues to parties to an economic transaction

words.name: deadweight loss
words.description: measure of lost economic efficiency

words.name: opportunity cost
words.description: when presented with multiple opportunities, the cost of making a certain choice in comparison to its alternatives

words.name: comparative advantage
words.description: in economics, the advantage one has over others in producing a particular good due to a lower relative marginal cost prior to trade

words.name: absolute advantage
words.description: economic concept

words.name: economies of scale
words.description: the cost advantages that enterprises obtain due to size, throughput, or scale of operation, with cost per unit of output generally decreasing with increasing scale as fixed costs are spread out over more units of output

words.name: diminishing returns
words.description: additional factor of production resulting in smaller increase in output

words.name: factors of production
words.description: the resources used in the production of goods and services, such as land, labour and capital

words.name: capital
words.description: assets used for the production of goods and services

words.name: entrepreneurship
words.description: creation or extraction of economic value

words.name: cost
words.description: value of money that has been used up to produce something

words.name: fixed cost
words.description: business expenses that are not dependent on the level of goods or services produced by the business

words.name: variable cost
words.description: costs that change in proportion to the good or service that a business produces

words.name: total cost
words.description: total economic cost of production

words.name: marginal cost
words.description: factor in economics

words.name: average cost
words.description: factor in economics

words.name: revenue
words.description: income that a business has from its normal business activities

words.name: marginal revenue
words.description: term used in microeconomics

words.name: profit
words.description: concept in economics

words.name: cost of goods sold
words.description: carrying value of goods sold during a particular period

words.name: gross margin
words.description: relating gross profits to net sales

words.name: profit margin
words.description: ratio between turnover and profit

words.name: markup
words.description: ratio of the cost of a good or service and its selling price

words.name: break-even point
words.description: equality of costs and revenues

words.name: contribution margin
words.description: cost accounting term

words.name: perfect competition
words.description: market structure in which firms are price takers for a homogenous product

words.name: monopoly
words.description: market structure with a single firm dominating the market

words.name: oligopoly
words.description: market form in which a market or industry is dominated by a small number of sellers

words.name: monopolistic competition
words.description: imperfect competition of differentiated products that are not perfect substitutes

words.name: market failure
words.description: situation in which the allocation of goods and services by a free market is not efficient and can be improved upon from the societal point of view, often leading to a net loss of economic value

words.name: externality
words.description: impact on any party not involved in a given economic transaction or act

words.name: public good
words.description: good that is non-excludable and non-rival

words.name: gross domestic product
words.description: market value of goods and services produced within a country

words.name: gross national product
words.description: total domestic and foreign output claimed by residents of a country, consisting of gross domestic product, plus factor incomes earned by foreign residents, minus income earned in the domestic economy by nonresidents

words.name: real gross domestic product
words.description: macroeconomic measure of the value of economic output adjusted for price changes

words.name: GDP deflator
words.description: measure of the level of prices of all new, domestically produced, final goods and services in an economy, with respect to a specific base year, but not based on a fixed basket of goods and services

words.name: consumption
words.description: purchase and use of goods and services

words.name: saving
words.description: the portion of income that is not spent on current consumption

words.name: investment
words.description: commitment of resources to achieve later benefits

words.name: government spending
words.description: government consumption, investment, and transfer payments

words.name: aggregate demand
words.description: total amount of demand for goods and services in an economy

words.name: aggregate supply
words.description: economic concept

words.name: consumer price index
words.description: indicates tracking prices of consumer goods as an economic measure

words.name: inflation
words.description: rise in price level in an economy over time

words.name: deflation
words.description: decrease in the general price level of goods and services

words.name: unemployment
words.description: state of being without work and actively seeking work

words.name: workforce
words.description: members of a particular organization or population who are able or eligible to work; labour pool in employment; it may also mean all those who are available for work

words.name: economic growth
words.description: measure of increase in market value of goods

words.name: business cycle
words.description: fluctuation in the degree of utilization of the production potential of an economy

words.name: recession
words.description: business cycle contraction

words.name: fiscal policy
words.description: use of government revenue collection and spending to influence the economy

words.name: monetary policy
words.description: subclass of the economic policy

words.name: money supply
words.description: component of macroeconomics

words.name: monetary base
words.description: portion of the commercial banks' reserves that are maintained in accounts with their central bank plus the total currency circulating in the public

words.name: velocity of money
words.description: rate of money changing hands

words.name: reserve requirement
words.description: type of regulation on commercial banks

words.name: money multiplier
words.description: ratio of money generated by banking system to central bank's monetary base

words.name: quantity theory of money
words.description: theory in monetary economics

words.name: interest rate
words.description: percentage of a sum of money charged for its use

words.name: nominal interest rate
words.description: concept in finance and economics

words.name: real interest rate
words.description: the interest rate after taking inflation into account

words.name: exchange rate
words.description: rate at which one currency will be exchanged for another

words.name: purchasing power parity
words.description: economic theory that states that the exchange rate between two countries is equal to the ratio of the currencies' respective purchasing power

words.name: balance of trade
words.description: difference between the monetary value of exports and imports

words.name: current account
words.description: concept in international economics

words.name: tariff
words.description: tax on the import and export of goods

words.name: subsidy
words.description: financial support to an economic sector

words.name: tax
words.description: mandatory financial charge or other type of levy imposed on a taxpayer (an individual or legal entity) by a governmental organization to collectively fund government spending, public expenditures, or to regulate and reduce negative externalities

words.name: marginal propensity to consume
words.description: metric in economics

words.name: marginal propensity to save
words.description: fraction of income increase that is saved

words.name: fiscal multiplier
words.description: the ratio of the change in aggregate demand to the change in government spending that caused it

words.name: Phillips curve
words.description: economic model illustrating an inverse relationship between inflation and unemployment

words.name: Laffer curve
words.description: representation of the relationship between taxation and government revenue

words.name: Lorenz curve
words.description: graphical representation of the distribution of income or of wealth

words.name: Gini coefficient
words.description: measure of inequality in income or wealth distribution

words.name: Okun's law
words.description: economic relationship

words.name: law of demand
words.description: principle in economics

words.name: law of supply
words.description: economic theory principle

words.name: Pareto efficiency
words.description: economic concept of a state in which no reallocation of resources can make everyone at least as well off

words.name: present value
words.description: economic concept denoting value of an expected income stream determined as of the date of valuation

words.name: future value
words.description: value of an asset at a specific date

words.name: net present value
words.description: valuation in finance

words.name: return on investment
words.description: ratio between the net profit and cost of investment resulting from an investment of some resources

words.name: return on equity
words.description: measure of the profitability of a business

words.name: return on assets
words.description: ratio to express the profitability of a company's assets in generating income

words.name: depreciation
words.description: allocating the cost of a (tangible) asset over a period of time

words.name: asset
words.description: economic resource, from which future economic benefits are expected

words.name: liability
words.description: a present obligation of an entity to transfer an economic benefit, shown on the balance sheet

words.name: equity
words.description: difference between the value of the assets/interest and the cost of the liabilities of something owned

words.name: debt
words.description: deferred payment, or series of payments, that is owed in the future

words.name: money
words.description: object or record accepted as payment

words.name: wage
words.description: the payment made by an employer to an employee in return for work performed

words.name: population
words.description: the number of people living in a given country or region

words.name: quantity
words.description: property that exists in a range of magnitudes or multitudes; property that can exist as a magnitude or multitude

words.name: GDP per capita
words.description: average income of an economy

words.name: rule of 72
words.description: methods of estimating the doubling time of an investment

words.name: doubling time
words.description: time required to double a quantity

words.name: example
words.description: a representative instance used to illustrate a rule or calculation

words.name: textbook
words.description: a book used as a standard work for the study of a subject

formulas.name: definition of GDP deflator
formulas.expression: "GDP deflator" = "gross domestic product" / "real gross domestic product" * 100

formulas.name: equation of exchange
formulas.expression: "money supply" * "velocity of money" = "price" * "real gross domestic product"

formulas.name: propensity identity
formulas.expression: "marginal propensity to consume" + "marginal propensity to save" = 1

formulas.name: doubling time by the rule of 72

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured

sources.name: economics textbook example
sources.description: illustrative figures used to demonstrate the calculation of an economic formula
